skill.name: create-mcp-skill
skill.description: Create MCP-based AI skills with documented tool discovery, command patterns, templates, and testing guidance.
skill.summary: Guides authors through creating AI skills that use Model Context Protocol servers.
skill.author: Cygnusfear

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many command, filesystem, network, and weak-crypto patterns in SKILL.md. Review found a documentation-only skill with no prompt injection or confirmed malicious intent, but it recommends running external MCP server commands, package runners, and broad process cleanup commands that require user review.

medium_findings.title: External MCP Server Command Execution Guidance
medium_findings.description: The skill provides copy-paste shell examples for installing or running MCP tooling and external MCP server packages. This is legitimate for an MCP authoring guide, but users could execute untrusted packages or unsafe SERVER_COMMAND values without review.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines contain direct command examples using package managers, mcp shell, bunx, npx, and user-replaced server commands. The intent is developer tooling, so the risk is real but not clearly malicious.

medium_findings.title: Broad Process Termination Pattern
medium_findings.description: Several examples recommend pkill -9 with a variable pattern before starting MCP shell sessions. A broad or incorrect pattern can terminate unrelated user processes and cause data loss.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command pattern is explicit and repeated in examples. It is framed as cleanup, but the -f pattern match and SIGKILL behavior create a concrete operational risk.

medium_findings.title: Broad Filesystem Server Example
medium_findings.description: The filesystem MCP server example uses the home directory as its target. If copied directly, that can expose more local files to the MCP server than a user intended.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line explicitly demonstrates a filesystem server pointed at the home directory. It is an example, so exploitation depends on user execution and server behavior.

low_findings.title: Relative Documentation Paths Flagged as Traversal
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged relative paths to documentation and reference skill files. These are Markdown links in documentation, not code that reads arbitrary user-controlled paths.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The locations are rendered documentation links and do not perform file access. No evidence found of user-controlled path traversal logic.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs Are Documentation References
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs are an example navigation target and links to MCP documentation or mcptools source. No evidence found that the skill sends data to these URLs.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs appear in documentation examples and resource links. There is no code in the skill that performs network exfiltration.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are Text Matches
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged weak cryptography at locations that contain description fields, template text, or parameter labels. No evidence found of cryptographic algorithm use or password hashing behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are documentation prose or placeholders, not cryptographic code. The alerts are consistent with substring matching rather than semantic crypto usage.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Command Templates
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill repeatedly documents Bash pipelines that run MCP shell sessions and external server commands.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command templates are direct shell examples. They are expected for this skill type, but they still require trusted inputs and package sources.

dangerous_patterns.title: SIGKILL Cleanup Pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: The recommended cleanup pattern uses pkill -9 with a pattern match before MCP shell execution.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The same termination pattern is repeated across examples. Its impact depends on the pattern value, but unsafe replacement values can terminate unrelated processes.

content.user_title: Create MCP Skills for AI Coding Agents
content.value_statement: Building an MCP skill requires clear command patterns, tool discovery, and safe documentation. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through MCP server exploration and skill template creation.
actual_capabilities: Guides MCP tool discovery with mcp tools and mcp shell.
Provides a standard skill directory and SKILL.md template structure.
Documents shell patterns that combine cleanup, command input, and timeouts.
Explains MCP parameter formatting and server flag placement.
Includes testing, troubleshooting, and performance checklists.
Links to MCP CLI documentation and reference implementation resources.
limitations: It does not install or verify any MCP server by itself.
It requires the MCP CLI and target server to be available.
Command examples must be reviewed before running on sensitive systems.
Generic placeholders must be replaced with trusted server names and paths.

use_cases.title: Build a New MCP Skill
use_cases.description: Create a skill folder, write SKILL.md, and document common MCP server workflows.

use_cases.title: Document MCP Server Tools
use_cases.description: Discover available tools, capture parameter requirements, and add tested command examples.

use_cases.title: Standardize Agent Workflows
use_cases.description: Create repeatable command patterns for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code skill users.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Basic MCP Skill
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for a known MCP server.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to create a basic MCP skill for a server I already trust. Include setup, quick start, and key tools sections.

prompt_templates.title: Explore Server Tools
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to inspect an MCP server command, list its tools, and draft documentation for required and optional parameters.

prompt_templates.title: Add Tested Workflows
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to add tested quick start workflows for common tasks, including timeouts and clear troubleshooting notes.

prompt_templates.title: Review an MCP Skill Design
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to review my MCP skill draft for command safety, parameter accuracy, documentation quality, and missing limitations.

output_examples.input: Create a skill for a trusted browser automation MCP server.
output: A skill folder outline with a SKILL.md structure.
A quick start workflow for opening a browser session.
A tool list section with parameter notes and troubleshooting guidance.

output_examples.input: Document the tools exposed by my MCP server.
output: A concise inventory of available tools.
Test notes that explain expected responses and timeout behavior.

output_examples.input: Review my MCP skill before publication.
output: A checklist of missing setup, testing, and limitation details.
Warnings about unsafe command patterns or broad filesystem access.
Suggested edits that make examples safer and easier to follow.

best_practices: Use only trusted MCP server packages and pin versions when possible.
Test each documented command in a controlled workspace before publishing.
Limit filesystem server paths to the smallest directory needed.
anti_patterns: Do not copy placeholder SERVER_COMMAND values into production workflows.
Do not use broad process patterns that can match unrelated services.
Do not expose a full home directory when a project folder is enough.

faq.question: What does this skill help create?
faq.answer: It helps create AI skills that use MCP servers through documented commands, templates, and testing guidance.

faq.question: Does it run an MCP server automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance and examples. The user or agent still chooses and runs the server commands.

faq.question: Which tools can use the generated skill?
faq.answer: The report lists Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as supported tools.

faq.question: Why is the audit risk medium?
faq.answer: The skill is documentation-only, but it recommends shell commands, external packages, and broad process cleanup patterns.

faq.question: How should users reduce risk?
faq.answer: Use trusted packages, pin versions, restrict filesystem paths, and review every command before execution.

faq.question: Can it document any MCP server?
faq.answer: It can guide documentation for many servers, but server-specific behavior must be tested separately.
